title: 3D Parallelism
existing_short_definition: Combining data parallelism, tensor parallelism, and pipeline parallelism to train frontier-scale models across clusters of GPUs.

title: Activation Steering
existing_short_definition: Modifying an LLM's residual stream activations at inference time to steer its behavior — e.g., making it more truthful, safer, or more on-topic.

title: Adapter (PEFT Adapter)
existing_short_definition: Small neural modules inserted inside a frozen base model's layers; only the adapter weights are trained.

title: Attention (Self-Attention)
existing_short_definition: The core mechanism in transformers that lets every token 'look at' every other token in the context and weight them by relevance.

title: AWQ (Activation-Aware Weight Quantization)
existing_short_definition: A 4-bit weight-only quantization method that scales weights based on their corresponding activation magnitudes, protecting important channels from quantization error.

title: Best-of-N Sampling
existing_short_definition: An inference-time strategy where the model generates N candidates and a reward model or verifier picks the best.
existing_code_example:
cands = [llm.complete(prompt, temperature=0.8) for _ in range(16)]
scores = [reward_model(prompt, c) for c in cands]
return cands[scores.index(max(scores))]

title: Chain of Thought
existing_short_definition: A prompting technique where you ask the AI to show its reasoning step-by-step before giving a final answer.

title: Constitutional AI
existing_short_definition: An alignment method from Anthropic where an AI critiques and revises its own responses against a written set of principles, reducing the need for human red-teamers.

title: Continued Pretraining
existing_short_definition: Extending the pretraining phase of a base model on domain-specific raw text, before any instruction tuning.

title: DARE (Drop And REscale)
existing_short_definition: A model merging method that randomly drops parameters from task vectors and rescales survivors, producing sparse merges.

title: Data Parallelism
existing_short_definition: The simplest distributed training: each GPU holds a full copy of the model and processes different shards of the batch, then gradients are averaged.

title: DeepSpeed ZeRO
existing_short_definition: Microsoft's memory-optimization technique that shards optimizer state (ZeRO-1), gradients (ZeRO-2), and parameters (ZeRO-3) across GPUs.

title: Distillation (Knowledge Distillation)
existing_short_definition: Training a small 'student' model to mimic a large 'teacher' model's outputs — trades a small quality loss for 5-50x inference cost reduction.

title: DoRA (Weight-Decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation)
existing_short_definition: A LoRA variant that decomposes weight updates into magnitude and direction components, consistently matching or beating LoRA.

title: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)
existing_short_definition: An RLHF alternative that fine-tunes an LLM on preference pairs (chosen vs rejected answers) without training a separate reward model.
existing_code_example:
# DPO with TRL library
from trl import DPOTrainer
trainer = DPOTrainer(
    model=model, ref_model=ref_model,
    beta=0.1,  # KL regularization strength
    train_dataset=preference_dataset,  # {prompt, chosen, rejected}
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
)
trainer.train()

title: Evol-Instruct
existing_short_definition: A synthetic instruction generation method that iteratively rewrites simple instructions into harder ones via explicit evolution operators.

title: Few-Shot Learning
existing_short_definition: Teaching an AI how to respond by giving it a few examples in the prompt, without retraining it.

title: Fill-in-the-Middle (FIM)
existing_short_definition: A code-model training objective that teaches the model to predict text between a prefix and suffix, enabling true code-completion inside existing files.

title: Fine-tuning
existing_short_definition: Fine-tuning is training an existing AI model on your specific data to make it better at your particular task.

title: FP8 Training
existing_short_definition: Training with 8-bit floating-point precision on supported hardware (H100+), doubling throughput over bf16 with careful scaling.

title: FSDP (Fully Sharded Data Parallel)
existing_short_definition: PyTorch's native technique for sharding model parameters, gradients, and optimizer state across GPUs to train models that do not fit on one device.

title: Full Fine-Tuning
existing_short_definition: Updating every parameter in a pretrained model during fine-tuning — maximum expressiveness at maximum cost.

title: GPTQ (Post-Training Quantization)
existing_short_definition: A post-training quantization method that reduces LLM weights to 3–4 bits using layer-wise error minimization — a widely used production quantizer.

title: Gradient Accumulation
existing_short_definition: Summing gradients over multiple small micro-batches before applying a single optimizer step, emulating a large batch on limited-memory hardware.

title: Gradient Checkpointing
existing_short_definition: A memory-saving technique that recomputes activations during the backward pass instead of storing them, trading compute for VRAM.

title: IA3 (Infused Adapter by Inhibiting and Amplifying Inner Activations)
existing_short_definition: A PEFT method that rescales activations with learned per-channel multiplicative vectors — extremely few trainable parameters.

title: Knowledge Editing
existing_short_definition: Surgically updating specific facts inside a trained model's weights without full fine-tuning — e.g., changing what it says the capital of a country is.

title: KTO (Kahneman-Tversky Optimization)
existing_short_definition: A preference-optimization method that trains on individual positive or negative signals rather than paired preferences, inspired by prospect theory.

title: Lookahead Decoding
existing_short_definition: A draft-model-free decoding speedup that uses Jacobi iteration to predict multiple tokens in parallel.

title: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)
existing_short_definition: A technique to fine-tune large language models by only training small 'adapter' matrices — 10-100x cheaper than full fine-tuning with nearly identical quality.
existing_code_example:
# Fine-tune Llama-3.2 with LoRA in 30 lines (Unsloth)
from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
    'unsloth/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-bnb-4bit', load_in_4bit=True)
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
    model, r=16, lora_alpha=32,
    target_modules=['q_proj','k_proj','v_proj','o_proj'])
# Now train — only LoRA weights update

title: Mechanistic Interpretability
existing_short_definition: The research program of reverse-engineering neural networks into human-understandable algorithms, circuit by circuit.

title: Medusa Decoding
existing_short_definition: A self-speculative decoding technique that adds multiple prediction heads to a single model, removing the need for a separate draft model.

title: MEMIT (Mass-Editing Memory in a Transformer)
existing_short_definition: An extension of ROME that edits thousands of facts simultaneously by distributing updates across multiple MLP layers.

title: Mixed Precision Training
existing_short_definition: Training with some tensors in low precision (fp16 or bf16) and others in fp32, roughly halving memory and doubling throughput.

title: Distillation (Knowledge Distillation, Reprise)
existing_short_definition: Training a smaller 'student' model to match a larger 'teacher' model's outputs — trading capacity for speed at similar quality.

title: Model Merging (Model Souping)
existing_short_definition: Combining the weights of multiple fine-tuned models into one by averaging or more sophisticated blending, often improving quality for free.

title: ORPO (Odds Ratio Preference Optimization)
existing_short_definition: A single-stage fine-tuning method that combines SFT and preference learning in one loss, eliminating the need for a separate DPO stage.

title: Outcome Reward Model
existing_short_definition: A reward model that scores only the final answer of a generation, not the intermediate steps.

title: PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning)
existing_short_definition: The umbrella term for fine-tuning methods that update only a tiny fraction of a model's parameters — LoRA, adapters, prefix tuning, IA3 — to save memory and compute.

title: Pipeline Parallelism
existing_short_definition: Splitting a model across multiple GPUs by assigning consecutive layers to different devices, streaming micro-batches through the pipeline.

title: PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization)
existing_short_definition: The RL algorithm historically used in RLHF to fine-tune an LLM against a reward model while constraining how far the policy drifts from the base.

title: Prefix Tuning
existing_short_definition: A parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that prepends a small set of trainable vectors to every transformer layer's key/value cache while freezing the base model.

title: Probing
existing_short_definition: An interpretability method that trains a small classifier on a frozen model's activations to test what information is linearly encoded.

title: Process Reward Model
existing_short_definition: A reward model that scores each intermediate step of a reasoning chain, not just the final answer — used to train and guide reasoning models.

title: Prompt Tuning
existing_short_definition: Fine-tuning only a small set of continuous vectors prepended to the input tokens, leaving the base model completely frozen.

title: Pruning (Neural Network Pruning)
existing_short_definition: Removing weights from a trained model to shrink it — either unstructured (zero individual weights) or structured (remove whole heads, channels, or layers).

title: Quantization-Aware Training (QAT)
existing_short_definition: Training a model with simulated quantization in the forward pass so the weights learn to be robust to the precision loss of actual quantization.

title: QLoRA (Quantized LoRA)
existing_short_definition: LoRA fine-tuning on a 4-bit quantized base model — lets you fine-tune a 70B model on a single 48GB GPU.
existing_code_example:
# QLoRA with bitsandbytes
from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig
bnb = BitsAndBytesConfig(
    load_in_4bit=True,
    bnb_4bit_quant_type='nf4',
    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,
    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,
)
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(base, quantization_config=bnb)

title: Quantization
existing_short_definition: Shrinking an AI model's file size by using lower-precision numbers, so it fits on less powerful hardware.

title: Rejection Sampling (Training)
existing_short_definition: A post-training technique where the model generates many candidates per prompt, a reward model or verifier picks the best, and only those are used for SFT.

title: RLAIF (Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback)
existing_short_definition: RLHF where an LLM, rather than a human, produces the preference labels used to train the reward model.

title: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)
existing_short_definition: A three-stage training method that makes an LLM follow human preferences by training a reward model from human rankings, then fine-tuning against that reward.

title: ROME (Rank-One Model Editing)
existing_short_definition: A knowledge-editing algorithm that changes a single fact inside an LLM via a rank-one update to a specific MLP layer's output matrix.

title: Self-Distillation
existing_short_definition: Distillation where the teacher and student are the same model size — often the same model at different checkpoints or ensembling with itself.

title: Self-Instruct
existing_short_definition: A method for bootstrapping instruction datasets by having an LLM generate new instructions and responses from a small seed set.

title: SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning)
existing_short_definition: The most basic fine-tuning method: show the model pairs of (instruction, ideal response) and train it to produce the response when given the instruction.
existing_code_example:
# SFT with TRL
from trl import SFTTrainer
trainer = SFTTrainer(
    model=model, tokenizer=tokenizer,
    train_dataset=ds,  # {'text': chat-templated string}
    max_seq_length=2048,
)
trainer.train()

title: SimPO
existing_short_definition: A reference-free preference optimization method with length-normalized reward, consistently matching or beating DPO at lower memory cost.

title: SLERP (Spherical Linear Interpolation)
existing_short_definition: A weight-blending method for model merging that interpolates along a sphere rather than linearly, preserving vector norms.

title: Sparse Autoencoders (Interpretability)
existing_short_definition: Autoencoders trained on LLM activations with a sparsity penalty, used to decompose dense activations into interpretable features.

title: Speculative Decoding
existing_short_definition: An inference speedup where a small fast 'draft' model predicts several tokens ahead, which the large target model then verifies in parallel.

title: Structured Pruning
existing_short_definition: Pruning that removes entire architectural units — heads, channels, layers — producing a smaller dense model that runs faster on any hardware.

title: Tensor Parallelism
existing_short_definition: Splitting individual matrix multiplications across multiple GPUs so each GPU computes a shard of the output.

title: TIES-Merging
existing_short_definition: A model merging method that resolves conflicts by trimming small-magnitude changes, electing signs, and disjoint-merging what remains.

title: Zero-Shot Learning
existing_short_definition: Asking an AI to do a task it hasn't seen examples of, relying purely on its pre-trained knowledge.
